item.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

item.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: In the coming weeks, Apple will move anonymously generated emails addresses to a different domain.

item.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

item.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said Tuesday that the company is working on over 40 different AI wearable devices — including jewelry, earbuds with cameras, pins, and watches — a sign of how aggressively the chipmaker is betting that the next major computing platform won't be a phone.

item.title: Bug in FIFA World Cup internal system gave anyone ability to modify TV stream
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A security researcher said a flaw in FIFA’s online platforms allowed her to access several internal systems, including one that could have allowed her to take control of the TV stream of every World Cup match.

item.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

item.title: Mobileye’s US robotaxi launch will put it on both sides of the AV business
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Mobileye apparently wants to own some of the robotaxi market, even if that puts it in direct competition with companies it supplies its self-driving system to.

item.title: Rivian cuts hundreds of workers after R2 deliveries start
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company said the cuts were part of a restructuring meant to help scale to profitability. Rivian recently pushed back its profitability goal to invest in autonomy.

item.title: Snap finally debuts its long-awaited AR glasses, Specs, and, oof, they aren’t cheap
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: For over a decade now, Snap has been working on this device. Now the glasses are finally here. So what stands out on first impression?

item.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

item.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Russell Brandom
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

item.title: India orders temporary ban on Telegram over exam fraud concerns
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The restrictions include a nationwide ban on Telegram until June 22 and a requirement to disable the app's message-editing feature.

item.title: Payments startup Flutterwave hits $3.2B valuation, backed by Ripple
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: African payments infrastructure company Flutterwave has hit a new valuation and landed blockchain company Ripple as an investor and partner.

item.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

item.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

item.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

item.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

item.title: This startup’s super metals could soon be in military drones, luxury watches, and chef’s knives
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Instead of heating metals, Foundation Alloy beats them into submission. The startup has raised $22 million to scale up production of its alloys.

item.title: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

item.title: Threads adds new personalization and community features as it reaches 500M monthly users
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The Meta-owned social platform announced a series of new features launching today, including a "Your Algo" tool that lets users control what they see in their feeds.
